summary: Colombian artist known for exaggerated, rotund figures. Most commonly identified by Abu Ghraib series (guards/dogs torturing prisoners), Death of Pablo Escobar, Mona Lisa Age 12, and Presidential Family. Dove of Peace sculpture replaced a sculpture destroyed by a bomb.

works.name: Abu Ghraib series (2005)
works.description: 56-piece series of guards and dogs torturing blindfolded, nude prisoners. Inspired by Seymour Hersh article in The New Yorker. Donated to Berkeley Museum of Art.

clues.clue: Series depicting guards and dogs torturing blindfolded nude prisoners at Abu Ghraib; inspired by Seymour Hersh article; donated to Berkeley
examples: painted dogs and guards torturing blindfolded, nude men in a series responding to the Abu Ghraib scandal
After reading a Seymour Hersh article in The New Yorker, he was inspired to paint a 56-piece series

works.name: Death of Pablo Escobar
works.description: Shows a giant man in white shirt standing on rooftops, riddled with bullets.

clues.clue: Giant man in white shirt standing on rooftops riddled with bullets; depicts a Colombian gangster's death
examples: a gangster falls backwards on a roof as bullets pierce his body
man standing on the roof of a house as bullets stream neatly through his body

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: How Botero is identified and other clues.

clues.clue: Colombian artist known for exaggerated, rotund/fat figures; emphasizes 'volume' and 'sensuality of form'
examples: Colombian artist known for exaggerated, rotund depictions of people

clues.clue: Dove of Peace sculpture: created from remains of Bird sculpture after it was blown up by a bomb
examples: created the sculpture Dove of Peace from the remains of the earlier sculpture Bird after it was blown up by a bomb

clues.clue: Mona Lisa, Age 12: parody of Mona Lisa with face filling a quarter of canvas; in namesake museum in Candelaria neighborhood
examples: namesake museum in the Candelaria neighborhood contains the painting Mona Lisa, Age 12

clues.clue: Presidential Family: self-portrait peeks from behind easel (alluding to Las Meninas); woman in pink with pelt, general, bishop
examples: Alluding to Las Meninas, a self-portrait peeks from behind an easel

clues.clue: Violence series: car bombings and kidnappings in Colombia
examples: Violence series depicts car bombings and kidnappings in his home country

clues.clue: Expelled from Jesuit high school for article praising Picasso's Cubism
examples: expelled from Jesuit high school for an article praising how Picasso's Cubist art destroyed individualism

clues.clue: Early still life of a mandolin with extremely small central hole credited with developing his style
examples: credited the development of this artist's style to an early still life of a mandolin with an extremely small central hole

clues.clue: Broadgate Venus: five-ton bronze nude in front of London's Liverpool Street Station
examples: His public sculptures include a five-ton bronze nude in front of London's Liverpool Street Station called Broadgate Venus

clues.clue: Died September 2023; eulogized by president Gustavo Petro
examples: president Gustavo Petro eulogized him after his September 2023 death

comprehensive_summary:
Fernando Botero was a Colombian artist known for his distinctive exaggerated, rotund figures, which he said emphasized 'volume' and 'sensuality of form.' His Abu Ghraib series (2005) is a 56-piece response to the torture scandal, showing guards and dogs violently humiliating blindfolded prisoners, inspired by a Seymour Hersh article in The New Yorker and donated to the Berkeley Museum of Art. The Death of Pablo Escobar shows a giant man on rooftops riddled with bullets.

His Mona Lisa, Age 12 parodies the original with a face filling a quarter of the canvas; it hangs in his namesake museum in Bogota's Candelaria neighborhood. The Presidential Family alludes to Las Meninas. He created the Dove of Peace sculpture from the remains of his Bird sculpture after it was destroyed in a terrorist bombing. He was expelled from Jesuit high school for praising Picasso. His Violence series depicts car bombings and kidnappings in Colombia. He died in September 2023.
